description: Molecule catalog for QEncode Suite v4. Same 7 molecules as v3 with cc-pVDZ basis (publication-grade). Active spaces are chemistry-driven and unchanged from v3. New v4 metadata: bk_tapering_supported, casscf_recommended, qubit counts. Geometry in Angstrom, PySCF format.
notes: Active spaces are identical to v3 — they reflect the chemically relevant orbital window, not the basis set.
BK mapping with tapering is only supported for H2 and HF in PL 0.45 (imaginary-strip verification passes). Larger molecules use JW + Parity only.

PAR/UCCSD is excluded for LiH, H2O, NH3, and N2 due to a known issue: UCCSD excitation operators generated in the JW basis are not correctly adapted for Parity tapering in these active spaces. BeH2 (D∞h linear symmetry) is the exception — PAR/UCCSD converges reliably. PAR/HEA and JW/both work correctly for all molecules.
N2 requires CASSCF orbital optimization (--orbital-opt casscf) to reliably certify. HF orbitals do not partition the [6e,6o] active space well for the triple bond.

entries.notes: Full valence active space. [2,2]: σ bonding + σ* antibonding. 4 qubits (JW) → 1 qubit after Z2 tapering. BK tapering verified clean (imaginary parts < 1e-6 × max_abs). All 6 entries (JW+PAR+BK × HEA+UCCSD) certified in v4 with gap ~1e-6 Ha.

[2,2] active space: F 2p HOMO + LUMO. 4 qubits (JW) → 1 qubit after Z2 tapering. BK tapering verified clean. All 6 entries certified in v4 with gap ~3.65e-4 Ha (residual from tapering approximation — well within 0.01 Ha certification threshold).

[4,4] active space: Li 2s + H 1s + polarization orbitals. 8 qubits (JW) → 5 after Z2 tapering. BK excluded: PL 0.45 introduces ~7 mHa imaginary artefacts in BK tapering for this active space — too large to safely strip. JW and Parity entries certified (JW/HEA gap 9.3e-5 Ha, JW/UCCSD gap 1.3e-4 Ha). PAR/UCCSD excluded (UCCSD operator basis mismatch with Parity mapping).

[4,4] active space: Be 2s + 2p + H 1s bonding/antibonding. Linear molecule (D∞h symmetry). BK excluded (same PL 0.45 imaginary artefact as LiH). All 4 supported entries (JW+PAR × HEA+UCCSD) certified in v4. BeH2 is the only [4e,4o]+ molecule where PAR/UCCSD converges reliably — the D∞h linear symmetry keeps the Parity-basis UCCSD operator space well-conditioned.

[4,4] active space: O lone pair + O-H bonding/antibonding. C2v symmetry. BK excluded (PL 0.45 imaginary artefact). PAR/UCCSD excluded. JW/HEA, JW/UCCSD, PAR/HEA all certified in v4. 3 certified entries.
[4,4] active space: N lone pair + N-H bond correlation. C3v symmetry. BK excluded (PL 0.45 imaginary artefact). PAR/UCCSD excluded. JW/HEA, JW/UCCSD, PAR/HEA certified in v4. 3 certified entries.

[6e, 6o] active space: σ, σ*, πx, πx*, πy, πy* — the full triple-bond manifold. D∞h symmetry. 12 qubits (JW). Strong multireference character. CASSCF orbital optimization required (--orbital-opt casscf): HF orbitals do not cleanly partition the triple-bond π/σ manifold in cc-pVDZ, leading to slow VQE convergence. CASSCF pre-optimises orbitals for the active space, making VQE certification achievable with 20 multistarts. BK excluded (PL 0.45 imaginary artefact). PAR/UCCSD excluded. Key DARPA QB-GSEE benchmark candidate.
